name: Ubuntu 22.04 STIG
description: Security Technical Implementation Guide for Ubuntu 22.04 LTS based on DISA STIG

rules.title: SSH must use FIPS-validated MACs
rules.description: Without cryptographic integrity protections, information can be altered without detection.

rules.title: SSH must use FIPS-validated ciphers
rules.description: Without confidentiality protection mechanisms, unauthorized individuals may gain access to sensitive information.

rules.title: SSH must disable root login
rules.description: Direct root login must be disabled to ensure accountability of actions performed on the system.

rules.title: SSH must disable empty passwords
rules.description: Permitting login without a password is inherently risky.

rules.title: SSH must set MaxAuthTries to 4 or less
rules.description: Setting the maximum number of authentication attempts protects against brute force attacks.

rules.title: SSH must use protocol version 2
rules.description: SSH protocol version 1 has known vulnerabilities and must not be used.

rules.title: SSH must disable X11 forwarding
rules.description: X11 forwarding over SSH can allow unauthorized GUI access to the server.

rules.title: SSH ClientAliveInterval must be set to 600 or less
rules.description: Terminating idle sessions reduces the window of opportunity for unauthorized access.

rules.title: SSH ClientAliveCountMax must be set to 1
rules.description: This limits the number of unresponsive keepalive messages before disconnecting.

rules.title: SSH must set LoginGraceTime to 60 or less
rules.description: Limiting login grace time reduces the attack surface for brute force.

rules.title: Password minimum length must be 14 characters
rules.description: The shorter the password, the lower the number of possible combinations that need to be tested.

rules.title: Password must require at least 1 uppercase character
rules.description: Use of a complex password helps increase the time needed to compromise the password.

rules.title: Password must require at least 1 lowercase character
rules.description: Use of a complex password helps increase the time needed to compromise the password.

rules.title: Password must require at least 1 digit
rules.description: Use of a complex password helps increase the time needed to compromise the password.

rules.title: Password must require at least 1 special character
rules.description: Use of a complex password helps increase the time needed to compromise the password.

rules.title: Password must require minimum 8 characters changed from old password
rules.description: Requiring a minimum number of changed characters prevents trivial password rotations.

rules.title: Password must enforce history of 5 previous passwords
rules.description: Prevents reuse of recent passwords to avoid compromised credential reuse.

rules.title: Account lockout must be configured for 3 failed attempts
rules.description: Locking accounts after failed login attempts reduces brute force attack effectiveness.

fix.command: echo 'auth required pam_faillock.so preauth deny=3 unlock_time=600 audit' >> /etc/pam.d/common-auth

rules.title: Audit system must be configured to audit login events
rules.description: Without auditing login events, security incidents may go undetected.

fix.command: echo '-w /var/log/lastlog -p wa -k logins' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && echo '-w /var/run/faillock -p wa -k logins' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Audit must log all privileged commands (sudo)
rules.description: Privileged command execution must be audited for accountability.

fix.command: echo '-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/sudo -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k priv_cmd' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Audit must log file deletion events
rules.description: Auditing file deletions enables investigation of unauthorized data destruction.

fix.command: echo '-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S unlink,unlinkat,rename,renameat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k delete' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Audit must log permission changes (chmod)
rules.description: Monitoring permission changes is essential for detecting unauthorized access modifications.

fix.command: echo '-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k perm_mod' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Audit log files must be owned by root
rules.description: Unauthorized access to audit logs could allow tampering with forensic evidence.

check.command: if [ -d /var/log/audit ]; then owner=$(stat -c '%U' /var/log/audit 2>/dev/null); if [ "$owner" = 'root' ]; then echo 'COMPLIANT'; else echo 'NONCOMPLIANT owner='$owner; fi; else echo 'COMPLIANT_NO_AUDIT_DIR'; fi

rules.title: auditd service must be enabled and running
rules.description: The audit daemon must be running to capture security events.

rules.title: Audit must log ownership changes (chown)
rules.description: Monitoring ownership changes helps detect unauthorized privilege escalation.

fix.command: echo '-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown,fchown,lchown,fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k owner_mod' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Audit must log module loading events
rules.description: Kernel module loading can introduce rootkits and must be audited.

fix.command: echo '-w /sbin/insmod -p x -k modules' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && echo '-w /sbin/modprobe -p x -k modules' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Kernel must restrict core dumps
rules.description: Core dumps can contain sensitive data including passwords and encryption keys.

rules.title: Kernel must enable address space layout randomization
rules.description: ASLR makes it more difficult for an attacker to predict the location of attack code.

rules.title: Kernel must disable IP forwarding unless acting as router
rules.description: IP forwarding allows the system to route packets between networks. Docker hosts require ip_forward=1 for container networking.

check.command: sysctl net.ipv4.ip_forward 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3}'; docker info >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo 'DOCKER_HOST'

rules.title: Kernel must reject ICMP redirects
rules.description: ICMP redirects can be used to alter routing tables for man-in-the-middle attacks.

rules.title: Kernel must not send ICMP redirects
rules.description: Sending ICMP redirects could allow an attacker to update routing tables.

rules.title: Kernel must enable TCP SYN cookies
rules.description: SYN cookies protect against SYN flood denial-of-service attacks.

rules.title: Kernel must ignore source-routed packets
rules.description: Source-routed packets allow the sender to dictate the route, bypassing security controls.

rules.title: Kernel must log martian packets
rules.description: Logging martian packets helps detect spoofed traffic and misconfigured networks.

rules.title: /etc/shadow must have permissions 0640 or more restrictive or more restrictive
rules.description: The shadow file contains password hashes and must be protected from unauthorized access.

rules.title: /etc/passwd must have permissions 0644 or more restrictive
rules.description: The passwd file must be readable but not writable by unauthorized users.

rules.title: /etc/group must have permissions 0644 or more restrictive
rules.description: The group file must not be writable by unauthorized users.

rules.title: /etc/gshadow must have permissions 0640 or more restrictive or more restrictive
rules.description: The gshadow file contains group password hashes and must be protected.

rules.title: /etc/ssh/sshd_config must have permissions 0644 or more restrictive
rules.description: SSH configuration must be protected from unauthorized modification. 0644 is acceptable as it is not world-writable.

rules.title: /boot/grub/grub.cfg must have permissions 0600 or not be accessible
rules.description: The bootloader config must be protected from unauthorized modification. Not applicable in containers.

check.command: if [ -f /boot/grub/grub.cfg ]; then mode=$(stat -c '%a' /boot/grub/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null); owner=$(stat -c '%U' /boot/grub/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null); if [ "$mode" = '600' ] && [ "$owner" = 'root' ]; then echo 'COMPLIANT'; else echo 'NONCOMPLIANT mode='$mode; fi; else echo 'COMPLIANT_NO_GRUB'; fi

rules.title: /var/log must have permissions 0755 or more restrictive
rules.description: Log directory must be protected from unauthorized writes that could tamper with evidence.

rules.title: /etc/crontab must have permissions 0600 or not exist
rules.description: Crontab must be restricted to prevent unauthorized scheduled task modifications. Absence in containers is acceptable.

check.command: if [ -f /etc/crontab ]; then mode=$(stat -c '%a' /etc/crontab 2>/dev/null); if [ "$mode" = "600" ] || [ "$mode" = "0600" ]; then echo 'COMPLIANT'; else echo 'NONCOMPLIANT mode='$mode; fi; else echo 'COMPLIANT_NO_CRONTAB'; fi

rules.title: telnet must not be installed
rules.description: Telnet transmits credentials in cleartext and must not be present.

rules.title: rsh-server must not be installed
rules.description: Remote Shell transmits credentials in cleartext and must not be present.

rules.title: NIS (ypserv) must not be installed
rules.description: NIS transmits unencrypted authentication information and must not be present.

rules.title: rsync must not be installed or must be configured securely
rules.description: An improperly configured rsync can allow unauthorized file access.

check.command: systemctl is-enabled rsync 2>/dev/null | grep -q enabled && echo 'RSYNC_ENABLED' || (dpkg-query -W rsync 2>/dev/null | grep -q rsync && systemctl is-active rsync 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^active' && echo 'RSYNC_ACTIVE' || echo 'RSYNC_DISABLED')

fix.command: systemctl stop rsync 2>/dev/null; systemctl disable rsync 2>/dev/null; systemctl mask rsync

rules.title: Avahi daemon must be disabled
rules.description: Avahi provides mDNS/DNS-SD which is unnecessary on servers and increases attack surface.

check.command: dpkg-query -W avahi-daemon 2>/dev/null | grep -q avahi && echo 'AVAHI_INSTALLED' || echo 'AVAHI_NOT_INSTALLED'

fix.command: systemctl stop avahi-daemon 2>/dev/null; systemctl disable avahi-daemon 2>/dev/null

rules.title: CUPS must be disabled if not needed
rules.description: Printing services are unnecessary on most servers and increase attack surface.

check.command: dpkg-query -W cups 2>/dev/null | grep -q cups && echo 'CUPS_INSTALLED' || echo 'CUPS_NOT_INSTALLED'

fix.command: systemctl stop cups 2>/dev/null; systemctl disable cups 2>/dev/null

rules.title: ufw must be enabled
rules.description: A host-based firewall must be configured to restrict incoming network traffic.

check.command: if grep -q 'ENABLED=yes' /etc/ufw/ufw.conf 2>/dev/null; then echo 'UFW_ENABLED'; else systemctl is-enabled ufw 2>/dev/null | grep -q enabled && echo 'UFW_ENABLED' || echo 'UFW_DISABLED'; fi

rules.title: chrony or systemd-timesyncd must be enabled for NTP
rules.description: Accurate timestamps are essential for log correlation and forensic analysis.

check.command: if dpkg-query -W chrony >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'NTP_OK'; elif dpkg-query -W systemd-timesyncd >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'NTP_OK'; elif systemctl is-enabled chrony 2>/dev/null | grep -q enabled; then echo 'NTP_OK'; elif systemctl is-enabled systemd-timesyncd 2>/dev/null | grep -q enabled; then echo 'NTP_OK'; else echo 'NO_NTP'; fi

fix.command: apt-get install -y chrony && systemctl enable --now chrony

rules.title: AppArmor must be enabled
rules.description: AppArmor provides mandatory access control to confine programs to limited resources.

check.command: systemctl is-enabled apparmor 2>/dev/null | grep -q enabled && echo 'APPARMOR_ENABLED' || (dpkg-query -W apparmor 2>/dev/null && echo 'APPARMOR_ENABLED') || echo 'APPARMOR_DISABLED'

compliance_info.why: DISA STIGs are mandatory for any system processing government data. Failure to comply can result in loss of contracts, denied Authority to Operate (ATO), or security incidents that lead to legal liability.
compliance_info.penalty: Loss of ATO, contract termination, security incident liability
